trivia_bullets.text: Appears in Commander Legends (CMR), released November 20, 2020 — the first draftable Commander set. Card originated in Commander 2014 (C14-54, 2014-11-07), illustrated by Ryan Alexander Lee, who has painted every major subsequent printing.

Over 40 distinct printings confirmed via Scryfall — appearing in nearly every Commander product from Commander 2014 through Lorwyn Eclipsed Commander (2026). Includes Commander 2014-2021, Starter Commander Decks, Commander Masters, Doctor Who, Warhammer 40K Commander, Secret Lair, Judge Promo, WPN promo.

EDHREC rank 44 out of the entire Commander card pool — format-defining staple. Produces any mana in the commander's color identity for {3}; sacrifices to draw a card. Universal utility regardless of color combination.

Artist Ryan Alexander Lee has illustrated every major printing continuously from 2014 through at least 2026 — one of the most artist-consistent reprints in Commander product history. Exceptions are the Warhammer 40K Commander faction-specific variants (three different artists for three different faction decks).
"It harmonizes with the essence of its master." Mirrors the card's mechanical design: it literally reads the commander's color identity to determine which mana it produces.

related_cards.name: Arcane Signet
related_cards.note: Direct functional competitor at {2}; has largely supplanted Sphere as go-to 2-drop rock

related_cards.name: Sol Ring
related_cards.note: Commander mana-rock apex; frequently cited alongside Sphere in format staples lists

related_cards.name: Mind Stone
related_cards.note: Earlier sac-draw rock; functional parallel at {2}

related_cards.name: Darksteel Ingot
related_cards.note: Earlier 3-mana any-color rock; predecessor in Commander staples lineage
